text: The surgeon uses a vessel sealer to take the middle colic vein and artery, demonstrating the transection line through the mesentery from the planned distal margin.

The surgeon splits the omentum over the planned margin of the transverse colon, preparing to resect it with the specimen. The camera has been switched from port three to two, allowing for better visualization of the small bowel mesentery and inferior dissection. The surgeon works with two right and one left instrument, joining the medially dissected plane.

The surgeon dissects through the gastrocolic omentum into the lesser sac, navigating around the lateral attachments near the hepatic flexure. The sponge marks the area of previous medial dissection. The right colon is fully mobilized, and the surgeon uses a stapler at the distal transverse colon and then staples the small bowel, with the camera positioned in arm two.
The surgeon positions the stapler at the stapled end of the colon to create an isoparastaltic anastomosis, ensuring the stapler fits properly from this angle. The anastomosis is then sewn closed.
